topic.what: Voting protocol achieving Holy Trinity of tally-hiding receipt-freeness and verifiability without trusted clients
topic.how: Combines Pedersen vector commitment re-randomization with sequential Audit-or-Cast cut-and-choose challenge and NIZK proofs
topic.why_matters: Addresses persistent trust gaps and privacy compromises in deployed election systems enabling public auditability without revealing votes

characteristics.evidence: Table II shows ACE is only protocol achieving all 8 properties
characteristics.context: Achieves Holy Trinity not attained by Helios Kryvos Ordinos DeVoS Selene etc

characteristics.what_it_measures: Probability of detecting cheating tallier per audit round
characteristics.evidence: Section V-A2 S3 cast-as-intended via Cut-and-Choose
characteristics.context: Sequential Audit-or-Cast reduces cheating probability to 2^-k

characteristics.vs_baseline: Requires at least one honest tallier for perfect hiding
characteristics.evidence: Section I: tallier-side re-randomization yields information-theoretic receipt-freeness
characteristics.context: Perfect hiding via Pedersen commitments eliminates need for shuffle proofs

characteristics.vs_baseline: O(1) for Helios Fasten DEMOS Prêt à Voter
characteristics.context: Trade-off for untrusted client assumption and tally-hiding
Linear complexity for tally aggregation phase

characteristics.evidence: Section IV-B threat model: at least one tallier must remain honest
characteristics.context: Privacy preserved as long as one tallier refuses to reveal shares

characteristics.what_itasures: Voter verification of correct vote recording
characteristics.evidence: Section V-A2 S3 and Audit-or-Cast mechanism Section III-E
characteristics.context: Enforced via sequential audit protocol under untrusted client assumptions

characteristics.what_itasures: Public does not learn intermediate or final tallies
characteristics.vs_baseline: Not achieved by Helios Fasten DEMOS Prêt à Voter DeVoS
characteristics.evidence: Section I and Theorem 2 3 privacy proofs
characteristics.context: Only Tdes learns final tally; no vote shares or margins revealed publicly

novelty: First protocol achieving Holy Trinity of tally-hiding receipt-freeness verifiability
Information-theoretic receipt-freeness via perfectly-hiding Pedersen commitments
Audit-or-Cast sequential cut-and-choose enforcing cast-as-intended under untrusted clients
Formal proof achieving all P1-P3 S1-S5 simultaneously without trusted clients
open_problems: Scalability to millions of voters with k audit rounds
Handling voter coercion via forced re-voting
